proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_080 (range 30-90, mean 65) are associated with longer pfs_months.
The ordinal variable feature_063 (levels 0, 1, 2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months such that higher levels correspond to shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson correlation between feature_080 and pfs_months is r=+0.7015 (p<1e-300). Quartile means of pfs_months across feature_080 quartiles increase monotonically: Q1 [30.0, 58.3]=2.50 mo; Q2 [58.4, 65.0]=4.09 mo; Q3 [65.1, 71.8]=5.27 mo; Q4 [71.9, 90.0]=6.96 mo. Direction is strongly positive and highly significant.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; groups=[df.loc[df.feature_063==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]]; stats.f_oneway(*groups)
analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA on feature_063 levels: F=2860.75, p<1e-300. Mean pfs_months: level 0 = 5.640 (n=17592); level 1 = 4.444 (n=24971); level 2 = 3.289 (n=7437). The trend is monotonically decreasing as feature_063 increases, supporting the hypothesis. Effect estimate is the level-2 minus level-0 mean difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_042=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_042=0.
Patients with feature_056=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_056=0.
Patients with feature_048=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_048=0.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_042==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_042==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_042=1 (n=17428) mean pfs_months=5.408 vs feature_042=0 (n=32572) mean=4.310. Difference = +1.098 months (p<1e-300). Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_056==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_056==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_056=1 (n=14969) mean=3.617 vs feature_056=0 (n=35031) mean=5.153. Difference = -1.536 months (p<1e-300). Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_048==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_048==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_048=1 (n=5090) mean=3.750 vs feature_048=0 (n=44910) mean=4.800. Difference = -1.050 months (p=1.45e-178). Direction matches hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_015=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_015=0.
Patients with feature_111=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_111=0.
Patients with feature_040=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_040=0.

Patients with feature_039=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_039=0 in unadjusted analysis, but the effect is markedly attenuated after adjusting for the strongest predictors (feature_080 and feature_063), consistent with confounding rather than an independent main effect.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_015==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_015==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_015=1 (n=17365) mean=4.328 vs feature_015=0 (n=32635) mean=4.887. Difference = -0.559 months (p=6.07e-144). Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_111==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_111==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_111=1 (n=34886) mean=4.865 vs feature_111=0 (n=15114) mean=4.297. Difference = +0.568 months (p=3.7e-143). Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_040==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_040==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_040=1 (n=9060) mean=4.317 vs feature_040=0 (n=40940) mean=4.776. Difference = -0.459 months (p=6.1e-70). Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039', data=df).fit(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039 + feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_042 + feature_056', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: feature_039=1 mean=4.824 vs =0 mean=4.462; difference = +0.362 mo (p=5.82e-59). After adjustment for feature_080, feature_063, feature_042, feature_056, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, the adjusted coefficient on feature_039 collapses to +0.014 (p=0.324, NS). The unadjusted association is thus largely explained by confounding with stronger prognostic features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_067 (range 0-24.6) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5, plausibly serum albumin) are associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_101 (range 1-100) are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_067'], df['pfs_months']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_067 + feature_080 + C(feature_063)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted Pearson r = -0.1155 (p=4.07e-148). Adjusted for feature_080 and feature_063, the OLS coefficient on feature_067 is beta = -0.0762 per unit (p<1e-300, t=-42.0). Direction matches and the effect is robust to adjustment.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_019'], df['pfs_months']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_019 + feature_080 + C(feature_063)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted Pearson r = +0.1002 (p=9.39e-112). In the multivariable model, beta on feature_019 = +0.476 per unit (p<1e-300, t=46.9). Effect is positive, large per unit, and robust to adjustment.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_101'], df['pfs_months']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_101 + feature_080 + C(feature_063)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted Pearson r = -0.0914 (p=2.98e-93). In the multivariable model, beta on feature_101 = -0.0189 per unit (p<1e-300, t=-45.1). Direction matches and effect is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of self-reported race/ethnicity in feature_011 (white, hispanic, black, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of insurance in feature_089 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.code: groups=[df.loc[df.feature_011==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df.feature_011.unique()]; stats.f_oneway(*groups)
analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_011: F is small, p=0.494. Mean pfs_months: white=4.679, asian=4.735, black=4.717, hispanic=4.707, other=4.762. Range across groups is only 0.083 months and the differences are not statistically significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.

analyses.code: groups=[df.loc[df.feature_089==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df.feature_089.unique()]; stats.f_oneway(*groups)
analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_089: p=0.818. Mean pfs_months: medicaid=4.706, private=4.685, medicare=4.692, uninsured=4.733. Range across groups is only 0.048 months. Hypothesis NOT supported - no detectable insurance-related disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the additional ordinal variables feature_021, feature_024, feature_127, feature_001, or feature_073 (each with five levels 0-4) shows a monotonic association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_021','feature_024','feature_127','feature_001','feature_073']: stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlations and ANOVAs across the five ordinal variables are all non-significant: feature_021 (r=+0.005, p=0.221; ANOVA p=0.705); feature_024 (r=-0.001, p=0.782; ANOVA p=0.592); feature_127 (r=+0.003, p=0.458; ANOVA p=0.638); feature_001 (r=-0.004, p=0.410; ANOVA p=0.825); feature_073 (r=+0.003, p=0.570; ANOVA p=0.270). The reported effect_estimate is the largest absolute Spearman r observed (feature_021 = +0.005). Hypothesis NOT supported - none of these ordinals appears prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable OLS model containing feature_080, feature_063, feature_042, feature_056, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, and feature_101, each of these predictors retains its expected sign on pfs_months and is independently statistically significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_042 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_034', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS on 50,000 patients (R^2=0.792). Coefficients with directions matching the hypothesized signs and all individually highly significant (each |t|>30, p<<0.001 unless noted): feature_080 +0.176/unit; feature_063 level 1 -1.189, level 2 -2.335 (vs level 0); feature_042 +1.102; feature_056 -1.549; feature_048 -0.984; feature_015 -0.582; feature_111 +0.531; feature_040 -0.471; feature_067 -0.077/unit; feature_019 +0.476/unit; feature_101 -0.019/unit. Two predictors entered tentatively did NOT survive adjustment: feature_039 (+0.014, p=0.324) and feature_034 (+0.002, p=0.872). Effect_estimate reported is overall model R^2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_042 and feature_056 on pfs_months: the harm from feature_056=1 is attenuated when feature_042=1 (i.e., feature_042 partially rescues the feature_056-positive subgroup).
The benefit of feature_042=1 (longer pfs_months) is larger in feature_063 level 0 patients than in feature_063 level 2 patients (i.e., feature_042 effect attenuates with higher feature_063).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_056', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_056: feature_042 main +1.096 (p<<0.001), feature_056 main -1.538 (p<<0.001), interaction term feature_042:feature_056 = +0.006 (p=0.901, t=0.13). The interaction is essentially zero - the two effects are additive and feature_042 does NOT differentially rescue feature_056-positive patients. Hypothesis NOT supported.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_063)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_063): feature_042 main effect in level 0 = +1.114 (p<<0.001). Interaction terms: feature_042:level1 = -0.021 (p=0.657), feature_042:level2 = +0.035 (p=0.604). Neither interaction is significant; feature_042's benefit is essentially homogeneous (~1.1 mo) across all three feature_063 strata. Effect_estimate reported is the largest interaction coefficient (level 2, +0.035). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between feature_056 and feature_080 on pfs_months: the harm associated with feature_056=1 grows larger (more negative) at higher values of feature_080.
There is a negative interaction between feature_042 and feature_080 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_042=1 shrinks at higher values of feature_080.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_056 * feature_080', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_056 * feature_080: feature_056 main = -0.825 (p<<0.001), feature_080 main = +0.180/unit (p<<0.001), interaction feature_056:feature_080 = -0.0112 per unit feature_080 (t=-6.97, p=3.1e-12). Direction matches hypothesis: each additional unit of feature_080 makes the feature_056-associated harm 0.011 month worse, so at feature_080=90 the marginal harm of feature_056=1 is roughly -0.825 + (-0.0112*90) = -1.83 months, vs roughly -1.16 months at feature_080=30. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_080', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_080: feature_042 main = +1.302, feature_080 main = +0.177, interaction feature_042:feature_080 = -0.0032 per unit (t=-2.02, p=0.044). Direction matches hypothesis (feature_042 benefit narrows by ~0.003 months for each unit increase in feature_080), but the magnitude is small: implied benefit at feature_080=30 is ~1.21 months; at feature_080=90 it is ~1.01 months. Statistically significant at conventional 0.05 threshold but borderline. Hypothesis weakly SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit associated with feature_042=1 is heterogeneous across the five feature_011 (race/ethnicity) categories - i.e., the treatment effect of feature_042 varies by race.

There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_080 and feature_019 on pfs_months: higher feature_019 (plausibly serum albumin) confers a steeper additional pfs_months benefit at higher feature_080 (likely older age).

analyses.code: for r in df.feature_011.unique(): stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[(df.feature_011==r)&(df.feature_042==1),'pfs_months'], df.loc[(df.feature_011==r)&(df.feature_042==0),'pfs_months']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_011, Treatment(reference="white"))', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified Welch t-tests of feature_042 effect within each race category yield: white +1.107 (p=1.6e-264), asian +1.062 (p=3.2e-23), black +1.060 (p=2.9e-45), hispanic +1.115 (p=5.6e-67), other +1.007 (p=3.7e-08). All four race x feature_042 interaction terms in the OLS model (asian, black, hispanic, other vs white as reference) have |t|<0.7 and p in [0.519, 0.903]; the largest absolute interaction coefficient is -0.100 (other vs white, p=0.545). The treatment effect of feature_042 is essentially homogeneous across race. Hypothesis NOT supported.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 * feature_019', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_080 * feature_019: feature_080 main = +0.157/unit, feature_019 main = +0.163/unit (p=0.117 alone), interaction feature_080:feature_019 = +0.0050 per unit-product (t=3.17, p=0.0015). Direction matches hypothesis - higher feature_019 amplifies the per-unit feature_080 benefit. Magnitude is small: at feature_019=2 the slope on feature_080 is 0.157 + 0.005*2 = 0.167/unit, at feature_019=5 it is 0.157 + 0.005*5 = 0.182/unit. Hypothesis SUPPORTED at p<0.01 albeit with modest magnitude.
